headline: Agent loops and production orchestration dominate the week as the field shifts from building agents to running them at scale.
body: Loops broke out as a leading technique alongside a wave of agent-construction tutorials, including a 30+ hour build walkthrough with a loop engineer template and a Model Context Protocol agent built on Google's Agent Development Kit. The center of gravity moved toward production: Databricks laid out a five-pillar framework covering evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance, grounded in a retail bank chatbot case that caught 47 PII breaches before launch. Multi-agent stacks combining LangGraph, MCP servers, and persistent memory appeared in real-world app builds, and new tooling entrants include Z AI's Z Code coding agent with a 5 million token daily allowance. On the breakout side, agent skills, scheduling, LLM-as-a-judge, continual pretraining, and prefill latency all surfaced as rising techniques.
